name: Two Ways with Acorn Squash
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Bring 1 quart of water to a simmer.
3. Cut the squash in half horizontally and clean out the seeds.
4. Place the cut-side down in a low-sided baking dish and fill with simmering water to about 1 inch.
5. Bake for 20 minutes.
For a vegetable presentation:
6. Turn the squash over and fill each half with the butter mixture.
7. Top with bacon slices, crossed.
8. Return to oven and cook until the tip of a sharp knife easily pierces the flesh, about 30-40 minutes.
9. Serve in the shells.
For timbales:
10. After the first 20 minutes in the oven, turn the squash over again and add 1 tablespoon of butter to each half.
11. Bake until they can easily be pierced with a sharp knife, about 30-40 minutes.
12. Carefully scoop out the flesh into a mixing bowl, reserving the shells.
13. Beat in remaining ingredients and eggs.
14. Place the mixture into the shells or timbales, and return to oven for 20-25 minutes or until set.
